title: Declare a Slice
instruction: Declare a slice of strings named `langs` containing `"go"`, `"python"`, and `"rust"`. Print the whole slice.

title: Update a Slice Value
instruction: Start with `scores := []int{80, 85, 90}`. Change the second value to `95`, then print the whole slice.
hint: The second value is at index 1, so use `scores[1] = 95`.

title: First and Last Slice Value
instruction: From `nums := []int{10, 20, 30, 40, 50}`, print the first value and then the last value.
hint: Use `nums[0]` for the first value and `nums[len(nums)-1]` for the last value.

title: Print Every Value
instruction: Loop through `[]int{3, 6, 9}` and print each value on its own line.
hint: Use a `for` loop with `i < len(nums)` and print `nums[i]`.

title: Find Min and Max
instruction: For `nums := []int{8, 3, 15, 2, 10}`, find the minimum and maximum values and print them on separate lines.
hint: Start with `min := nums[0]` and `max := nums[0]`, then compare the rest in a loop.

title: Sum Slice Elements
instruction: Sum the values in `[]int{1, 2, 3, 4, 5}` and print the total.
hint: Use `sum := 0` and add each value inside a loop.
